name.description: A short human readable and descriptive name.

datetime.description: The date and time when this activity was started.

lab_id.description:
An ID string that is unique at least for the lab that produced this
data.

description.description: Any information that cannot be captured in the other fields.

method.description: A short consistent handle for the applied method.

steps.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference. An ordered list of all the dependant steps that make up this activity.

name.description: A short and descriptive name for this step.

start_time.description:
Optionally, the starting time of the activity step. If omitted, it is assumed to
follow directly after the previous step.

comment.description: Any additional information about the step not captured by the other fields.

duration.description: The duration time of the process step.

end_time.description: The date and time when this process was finished.

instruments.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference. A list of all the instruments and their role in this process.

items.description: A section used for referencing an Instrument.

name.description: A short descriptive name for the role of this reference.

reference.description: A reference to a NOMAD `Instrument` entry.

lab_id.description: The readable identifier for the entity.

samples.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference. The samples as that have undergone the process.

items.description: A section used for referencing a CompositeSystem.

name.description: A short descriptive name for the role of this reference.

reference.description: A reference to a NOMAD `CompositeSystem` entry.

lab_id.description: The readable identifier for the entity.

batch.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference.

layer_material_name.description: The chemical formula using common abreviations. This will be used to set layer material, if not set, e.g. MAPbI3.

layer_material.description: The chemical formula of the layer. This will be used directly and indirectly in the search. The formula will be used itself as well as the extracted chemical elements.

layer_thickness.description: The thickness of the sample, either measured or assumed.

layer_transmission.description: Percentage of light that gets transmitted through the layer.

layer_morphology.description: A description of the morphology of the film

layer_chemical_id.description: Identifier of the product used for creating the layerin case it is purchased from supplier

product_number.description: Product Number, as defined by materials sent by the suppliers

lot_number.description: LOT, Experiment or Batch Number, as defined by suppliers

supplier.description: Partner/Company that supplies the product to the end user

solution.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference.

solution_viscosity.description: Viscosity of the ink solution, critical for printability in inkjet printing

solution_contact_angle.description: The angle formed between the solution and the underlying layer.

solution_surface_tension.description: The surface tension of the solution.

name.description: A short human readable and descriptive name.

datetime.description: The date and time associated with this section.

lab_id.description:
An ID string that is unique at least for the lab that produced this
data.

description.description: Any information that cannot be captured in the other fields.

elemental_composition.description:
A list of all the elements found in the system together and their respective
atomic fraction within the system.

items.description: A section for describing the elemental composition of a system, i.e. the element and its atomic fraction.

element.description: The symbol of the element, e.g. 'Pb'.

atomic_fraction.description:
The atomic fraction of the element in the system it is contained within.
Per definition a positive value less than or equal to 1.

mass_fraction.description:
The mass fraction of the element in the system it is contained within.
Per definition a positive value less than or equal to 1.

components.description:
A list of all the components of the composite system containing a name, reference
to the system section and mass of that component.

items.description: A section for describing a component and its role in a composite system.

name.description: A short name for the component.

mass_fraction.description: The mass fraction of the component in the composite system.

chemical.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference.

concentration_vol.description: Volume-to-volume concentration (v/v), e.g. for liquid additives or solvents.

chemical_2.description: A section for pure substances existing as "compounds" in the PubChem database and for tracking substances provided by suppliers

name.description: A short name for the substance.

molecular_mass.description:
The mass of the most likely isotopic composition for a single molecule,
corresponding to the most intense ion/molecule peak in a mass spectrum.

molar_mass.description:
The molar mass is the sum of all atomic masses of the constituent atoms in a
compound, measured in g/mol. In the absence of explicit isotope labelling,
averaged natural abundance is assumed. If an atom bears an explicit isotope label,
100%% isotopic purity is assumed at this location.

monoisotopic_mass.description:
The mass of a molecule, calculated using the mass of the most abundant isotope of
each element.

product_number.description: Product Number, as defined by materials sent by the suppliers

lot_number.description: LOT, Experiment or Batch Number, as defined by suppliers

supplier.description: Partner/Company that supplies the product to the end user

product_number.description: Product Number, as defined by materials sent by the suppliers

lot_number.description: LOT, Experiment or Batch Number, as defined by suppliers

supplier.description: Partner/Company that supplies the product to the end user

solution.description: ENTRY REFERENCE: this field must hold a reference to another NOMAD entry, never a plain name or free text. Omit it unless the text explicitly provides such a reference.

solvent_ratio.description: The ratio of the solvents, as stated (e.g. "1:6").

institute.description: Alias/short name of the home institute of the owner, i.e. *HZB*.

owner.description:
Alias for the owner of the identified thing. This should be unique within the
institute.

datetime.description:
A datetime associated with the identified thing. In case of an `Activity`, this
should be the starting time and, in case of an `Entity`, the creation time.

short_name.description:
A short name of the the identified thing (e.g. the identifier scribed on the
sample, the process number, or machine name), e.g. 4001-8, YAG-2-34.
This is to be managed and decided internally by the labs, although we recommend
to avoid the following characters in it: "_", "/", "\" and ".".

lab_id.description:
Full readable id. Ideally a human readable id convention, which is simple,
understandable and still have chances of becoming unique.
If the `owner`, `short_name`, `ìnstitute`, and `datetime` are provided, this will
be formed automatically by joining these components by an underscore (_).
Spaces in any of the individual components will be replaced with hyphens (-).
An example would be hzb_oah_20200602_4001-08.

solvent_ratio.description: The ratio of the solvents, as stated (e.g. "1:6").

institute.description: Alias/short name of the home institute of the owner, i.e. *HZB*.

owner.description:
Alias for the owner of the identified thing. This should be unique within the
institute.

datetime.description:
A datetime associated with the identified thing. In case of an `Activity`, this
should be the starting time and, in case of an `Entity`, the creation time.

short_name.description:
A short name of the the identified thing (e.g. the identifier scribed on the
sample, the process number, or machine name), e.g. 4001-8, YAG-2-34.
This is to be managed and decided internally by the labs, although we recommend
to avoid the following characters in it: "_", "/", "\" and ".".

lab_id.description:
Full readable id. Ideally a human readable id convention, which is simple,
understandable and still have chances of becoming unique.
If the `owner`, `short_name`, `ìnstitute`, and `datetime` are provided, this will
be formed automatically by joining these components by an underscore (_).
Spaces in any of the individual components will be replaced with hyphens (-).
An example would be hzb_oah_20200602_4001-08.

annealing.description: Base class for annealing of a sample

gas.description: The gas used for gas quenching, e.g. nitrogen.

air_knife_angle.description: The angle of the air knife.

air_knife_distance_to_thin_film.description: The distance of the air knife to the thin film.

sintering.description: Base class for sintering of a sample

items.description: Base class for sintering of a sample
